nodes.en: Dictionaries are repositories mapping words to definitions, etymologies, and usage examples within a language. They preserve semantic precision and document how meaning shifts across historical periods.
nodes.cs: Slovníky jsou úložiště mapující slova na definice, etymologie a příklady použití v rámci jazyka. Zachovávají sémantickou přesnost a dokumentují, jak se značení mění v čase.
nodes.brief:
Dictionaries are repositories mapping words to definitions, etymologies, and usage examples within a language. They preserve semantic precision and document how meaning shifts across historical periods, serving as the arbiter of correct usage that other reference works assume rather than establish.

Within [[09]] Reference & Documentation, dictionaries are the semantic counterpart to [[09.01]] Encyclopedias: where an encyclopedia explains a concept, a dictionary fixes the word for it, and the two are frequently consulted together when a reader meets unfamiliar terminology. Dictionaries also underpin [[09.05]] Standards & Specifications, since a specification's precision depends on terms whose meaning is fixed and shared — a standards body cannot define tolerances on a word that means different things to different readers.

For an authoritative historical dictionary of English, see the [Oxford English Dictionary](https://www.oed.com/), and for a freely available general dictionary, [Merriam-Webster](https://www.merriam-webster.com/).
